findings.problem: The chapter's central power conflict peaks when Ravi pushes Anika to falsify pain data, but the beat resolves too quickly into compliance and a stabilizing close. That rapid de-escalation weakens forward causal pressure after promising a harder authority struggle.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the closing conflict in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:49, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:53 so Anika's truthful report still stands, but Ravi's resistance leaves an explicit unresolved consequence (behavioral, relational, or procedural) that carries into the next chapter.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:47-53 ends with one concrete unresolved risk/action tied to Ravi's opposition after the report is submitted; FAIL if the same span ends in full interpersonal settlement plus institutional reassurance.

findings.problem: Sanitization drift: a coercive parent-child moment (pressure to underreport medical risk) is blunted by immediate softening and a neat compliance payoff. The narrative installs a safety valve instead of dwelling in the full discomfort and cost of the coercion attempt.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:49, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:53 to keep concrete stakes live after Anika enters truthful numbers: show immediate interpersonal or practical fallout before any partial cooling, and remove interpretive language that reassures the reader too quickly.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised lines show at least one concrete consequence of Ravi's coercion attempt (stated retaliation, withdrawal, or imposed condition) before scene end, and the final beat preserves unresolved discomfort; FAIL if the same section still resolves with a comforting or triumphant release beat.

findings.problem: Timidity/summary replaces scene specificity at a critical pressure point: the narration says Ravi's lowered voice made 'the threat clearer' but does not concretize the threat mechanism in that beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:31 so the threat is rendered through explicit language, gesture, or immediate consequence cue instead of abstract narrator labeling.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:31 contains a specific threatening action or verbal condition that a reader can identify without narrator explanation; FAIL if the beat still relies on abstract phrasing like 'the threat' without concrete manifestation.

findings.problem: Procedural exposition crowds the dramatic line in the chapter's back half: protocol and legal wording run in long blocks, reducing embodied consequence density during a scene that should escalate through behavior.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and redistribute policy delivery across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:29, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:41, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:43 by inserting immediate reaction beats (body language, interior calculation, or action) after each key rule statement.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if in revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:29-43 no more than two consecutive sentences are pure policy without an adjacent character reaction beat; FAIL if multi-sentence policy blocks still stand without embodied interruption.

findings.problem: Dialogue register drifts stiff in high-pressure exchanges via repeated uncontracted forms (e.g., 'She is', 'You are', 'We are', 'They are') despite a high-contraction default and contemporary spoken context.
findings.rewrite_direction: Adjust Ravi's speech rhythm at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:13, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:19, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:45 toward natural contraction-heavy delivery, keeping any retained full-form phrasing only where emphasis or deliberate precision is explicit.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised Ravi dialogue in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:13, :19, and :45 uses contractions in at least 3 of 5 eligible auxiliary slots and any remaining uncontracted form reads as deliberate emphasis; FAIL if multiple unmotivated full forms remain.

findings.problem: Tess and Marisol converge toward a similar institutional compliance cadence, weakening idiolect separation in adjacent authority scenes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate voice design between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:29 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:37-43: keep Tess in body-process/actionable rehab language and give Marisol stricter systems-and-limitations diction with a distinct sentence rhythm.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a blind read of revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:29 and :37-43 can attribute each speaker by lexical and rhythmic cues without dialogue tags; FAIL if both still read as interchangeable generic compliance voice.

findings.problem: Recurring negation construction appears more than twice and starts to read as a tic (e.g., 'did not/was not' pivots), flattening late-chapter cadence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast selected negation beats in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:45, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:49, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:51 into affirmative, image-led clauses while preserving meaning and tension.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:45-51 contains no more than one stand-alone negation-formula sentence and introduces at least two affirmative sensory/action clauses in those same beats; FAIL if repeated negation pivots still dominate the close.

findings.problem: A repeated simile template ('like/as if' comparison) appears in quick succession more than twice, making the descriptive texture feel mechanically patterned.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:21, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:37, keep the strongest comparison and convert at least two others into direct concrete observation.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two cited simile constructions are replaced with direct sensory description while preserving scene information; FAIL if the same span still relies on three or more 'like/as if' comparison beats.

summary: Chapter 10 lands core continuity beats and institutional stakes, but this review remains FAIL across all lenses due to unresolved craft risks: sanitized de-escalation at the coercion climax, abstracted threat rendering, policy-heavy exposition blocks, dialogue stiffness from contraction drift, and recurring prose formulas that flatten cadence.
